lines.text: Well. That was certainly... something from our very own Quinn Foley. Thank you, Quinn, for that... spirited report.
[pause] Close your eyes. If you're in a room with the lights off, good. If you aren't, turn them off. I'll wait.
[pause] Good. We're here. You're here. Whatever is happening outside that door is outside that door.
Breathe in through your nose for four counts. One. Two. Three. Four.
And release through your mouth. Slow. Let it fall out of you like you're setting something down.
Again. In for four. Feel your ribs expand. Feel the floor under you. You are a body in a room. That's all you are right now.
Now place your hands on your knees. Palms up. You're not holding anything. You're not carrying anything. For the next forty minutes you have no responsibilities to anyone.
In for four. Notice the cool air entering. Notice it's clean. The filtration is working. Tim checks the filters. Tim always checks the filters.
And release. Feel the warmth leaving you. You're giving heat back to the room. The room takes it. The room doesn't judge it.
One more. Breathe in and picture a place that is yours. Not shared. Not borrowed. Not contested. A place no one else has a key to.
In again. Deeper this time. Picture the house you grew up in. The one with the kitchen that always smelled like sauce on Sundays. You know the one.
Notice who's sitting at the head of the table. Notice it isn't you. It was never going to be you.
Breathe in. Think about the last time you were invited to that table. Think about who did the inviting. Think about the tone. The tone that says you're welcome here but means I'm allowing this.
Hold. Let that sit in your chest.

Breathe in. Your brother got the house. Breathe out. He didn't even want it. He got the house because he was the son and you were the other thing. The thing that makes Sunday sauce but doesn't get the kitchen.
In again. Remember the conversation. The lawyer's office. The paper that said his name where yours should've been. Remember how calm you were. Remember how everyone said you took it so well.

Breathe in. Picture him in that house right now. Right now, tonight. He's in the living room. He changed the carpet. He changed your mother's carpet. He put in hardwood because his wife read an article.
Notice your hands. Are they fists? Open them. Palms up. We don't hold things here.
Breathe in. Forgiveness is a practice. It is not a moment. It is a practice you do every day like brushing your teeth, and some days you spit blood, and that's fine. That's the practice.
You're a body in a room. That's all you are right now. That's enough.
This is Sister Marigold, and I'll be with you until midnight. Palms up. We're breathing.

tickerItems.text: You are a body in a room. That's all you are right now.
East Side (Atwood) reports +6 kill count spike 22:45–23:00. Concentrated near Olbrich corridor.
Text from Maple Bluff listener: 'Did she just say what I think she said?'
Forgiveness is a practice. Some days you spit blood. That's the practice.
Willy Street neighborhood watch confirms perimeter holding at Yahara Bridge. Armed positions maintained.
Text from Tenney Park listener: 'Marigold please keep going. I need this tonight.'
The thing that makes Sunday sauce but doesn't get the kitchen.
